stemHtml: Select the option that best describes “nose-wheel shimmy”.

options.a: An oscillatory longitudinal movement of the nose-wheel about the lateral axis of the aircraft caused by the landing gear being lowered prior to landing or retracted after take-off.
options.b: An oscillatory lateral movement of the nose-wheel about the longitudinal axis of the aircraft caused by the landing gear being lowered prior to landing or retracted after take-off.
options.c: An oscillatory lateral movement of the nose-wheel about the longitudinal axis of the aircraft when the nose wheel is in contact with the runway during the take-off or landing run.
options.d: An oscillatory longitudinal movement of the nose-wheel about the lateral axis of the aircraft when the nose wheel is in contact with the runway during the take-off or landing run.
